response: Emily divides the 24 residents into groups of 6, resulting in 4 groups. Each group receives 3 initial writing prompts, so 4 groups × 3 prompts = 12 prompts. She also has 10 extra prompts to be shared equally among the 4 groups, which means each group gets 10 ÷ 4 = 2.5 prompts. Since prompts are indivisible, Emily will need to round up to ensure every group has access to the extra prompts. Therefore, she should prepare 3 extra prompts per group, totaling 4 × 3 = 12 extra prompts. Adding the initial prompts, the total number of prompts Emily needs to prepare is 12 + 12 = 24. The library of prompts will be well-utilized, and the library will support the residents in their creative endeavors. The END.
